skill.name: wordpress-admin
skill.description: Manage WordPress pages, Yoast SEO metadata, media workflows, and local Docker administration using WP-CLI examples and helper scripts.
skill.summary: Automates WordPress page and SEO administration for a specific local and production setup.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static command, network, filesystem, and credential findings are mostly true positives in context. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, but hardcoded production credentials and shell-based WP-CLI execution make this unsafe to publish without remediation.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded Production Credentials
high_findings.description: The skill documents site-specific production access details, including a WordPress site, FTP host, FTP user, and an lftp command containing a hardcoded password. Publishing this skill could expose credentials and enable unauthorized changes to the referenced production WordPress site.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The FTP login command contains a literal username and password for the production host. The surrounding instructions describe production sync and theme upload workflows.

high_findings.title: Shell Command Injection Risk in WP-CLI Helpers
high_findings.description: The Python helpers construct shell command strings from CLI arguments and execute them with shell=True. Inputs such as title, slug, content, template, post ID, and SEO values are not passed as argv arrays, so crafted values could execute unintended shell syntax on the host.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run is invoked with shell=True, and command fragments include user-controlled arguments. The local escaping is partial and does not provide shell-safe argument boundaries.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Site-Specific Administration Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill is built around a named production domain, local Docker container, and commands that create, update, delete, install plugins, reset databases, and upload theme files. These are legitimate admin tasks, but marketplace publication would expose operational procedures for one specific site.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced operations are explicitly administrative and target named local and production resources. They are not generic examples.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Secret File Dependencies
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to store external API keys in root-owned hidden files and use them in network requests. This can be legitimate, but it requires careful permissions and should not be bundled with real keys or broad file access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly reference hidden files under /root for Pexels and Unsplash API keys. No actual stock-photo API key value was found in the reviewed lines.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Mostly Functional Configuration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded WordPress, REST API, documentation, and stock-photo URLs are expected for this administration skill. They are still relevant because several URLs point to a specific production site rather than placeholders.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible configuration and reference links, not evidence of exfiltration. The production domain makes them operationally sensitive.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner appears to match terms such as keyphrase, meta keys, API key labels, and SEO field names. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found SEO key names and API-key documentation, not cryptographic primitives. The listed weak-crypto alerts do not map to actual crypto use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Code Execution Plus Networked Administration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines Bash, Docker, curl, lftp, Python subprocess execution, production endpoints, and credential handling. This combination is powerful for operations but risky for a public marketplace skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly allow and demonstrate command execution against networked WordPress and FTP targets. The risk is confirmed even without malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Administrative Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflows include deleting WordPress pages and resetting the local database volume. These actions can cause data loss if run against the wrong environment or reused without safeguards.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delete and reset commands are explicit and operational. The risk depends on environment selection, so this is a dangerous pattern rather than confirmed misuse.

content.value_statement: WordPress administrators need repeatable ways to create pages and update SEO metadata. This skill provides WP-CLI workflows and helper scripts for a specific Docker-based setup.
actual_capabilities: Create, list, retrieve, update, and delete WordPress pages through local WP-CLI helpers.
Set Yoast focus keyphrase, meta description, SEO title, canonical, and robot metadata.
Provide command examples for plugin checks, media imports, featured images, and page templates.
Document Docker commands for starting, stopping, logging, and resetting a local WordPress environment.
Document FTP-based theme file upload for the referenced production WordPress site.
limitations: The Python scripts return "REST API not implemented yet" for production REST operations.
The workflows are specific to one local Docker container and one production domain.
The referenced wp-media.py helper is documented but not present in the scanned file structure.
The scripts require trusted inputs because shell command construction is not safely isolated.

use_cases.title: Build standard site pages
use_cases.description: Create pages with slugs, templates, status values, and optional content in a local WordPress Docker environment.

use_cases.title: Maintain SEO metadata
use_cases.description: Update Yoast fields for existing posts or pages and generate short meta description drafts.

use_cases.title: Operate a local WordPress stack
use_cases.description: Run documented Docker, plugin, media, and WP-CLI maintenance commands for a known development environment.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner page creation in the local Docker site.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WordPress page titled "About Us" with slug "about-us" on the local site. Keep it as a draft.

prompt_templates.title: Update Yoast metadata
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate SEO update for an existing page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set Yoast SEO metadata for post ID 42. Use focus keyphrase "Miami real estate", a concise meta description, and an SEO title under 60 characters.

prompt_templates.title: Review page operations
prompt_templates.prompt: List current WordPress pages on the local site, then get details for the page with slug "privacy-policy".

prompt_templates.title: Plan a safe publishing workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a safe workflow to create a terms page locally, verify its SEO metadata, and describe what must be checked before any production upload.

output_examples.input: Create a privacy policy page with a custom template and SEO metadata.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the local site, creates the page, applies the template, then reports the page ID and SEO fields that were set.

output_examples.input: Generate a meta description for an existing services page.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a short search-friendly description, checks the length, and notes whether it fits common Yoast guidance.

output_examples.input: Check the WordPress pages in the Docker environment.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the page listing workflow and summarizes each page by ID, title, status, and slug.

best_practices: Replace site-specific credentials with environment variables or a managed secret store before reuse.
Run page and SEO changes in a local or staging environment before production deployment.
Review every generated command before execution when content, slugs, templates, or IDs come from user input.
anti_patterns: Do not publish this skill with real production hostnames, usernames, passwords, or private paths.
Do not pass untrusted text directly into the helper scripts without shell-safe argument handling.
Do not run destructive commands such as forced deletes or database resets without a verified backup.

faq.question: Does this skill support production WordPress REST publishing?
faq.answer: The documentation includes REST examples, but the provided Python scripts report that REST API support is not implemented.

faq.question: Can it manage Yoast SEO fields?
faq.answer: Yes. The SEO helper can set and read several Yoast post meta fields through WP-CLI on the local Docker site.

faq.question: Does it include a media upload script?
faq.answer: The skill documentation references wp-media.py, but that file is not present in the scanned file structure.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: No. The audit found hardcoded production credentials and shell command injection risk that must be fixed first.

faq.question: What tools does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects WP-CLI through Docker, curl, Python scripts, and lftp for the documented workflows.

faq.question: Who is the best fit for this skill?
faq.answer: It fits administrators who control the referenced WordPress environment and can safely manage credentials and command execution.
